name: Tortelli Maremmani
description: Square filled pasta from the Tuscan Maremma, traditionally filled with spinach or nettles, local cheese, ricotta, and spices. They are cut with a wheel-type pasta cutter into generous squares.
instructions: Sift flour onto a board and knead for a long time with eggs until the dough is firm and smooth. Let rest.
Roll out the dough into a thin sheet with a rolling pin.
Cut the sheet into strips about 5 inches (12 cm) wide.
Place well-spaced hazelnut-sized piles of filling on half of each strip.
Fold the other half of the strip over the filling and press closed around each mound.
Use a wheel-type pasta cutter to cut crosswise at intervals of about 2.5 inches to make square ravioli.
Let dry briefly, then boil a few at a time in salted water.
history: Tortelli maremmani are traditional to the Maremma grossetana, the strip of Grosseto Province near the coast, but also appear in the hills and areas surrounding Monte Amiata. Historically filled with a 'poor' filling of spinach or nettles, ricotta, and local cheese, they were served with hearty meat ragu. In recent years, as economic conditions have improved, the richer meat-filled tortello has become more common, while the traditional vegetable-filled version has slowly declined.
source_books: The Encyclopedia of Pasta: Over 350 Recipes
